comments.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × this child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), cross-checked against the full sibling roster.

This child is the OWNER of parent AC5 ("Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically; every 'expected-bug' probe guard is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes") and parent SC4 ("Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted, and no probe encodes a bug as expected"). Both are FULLY covered:
- 822a AC1 → hermetic bidirectional round-trip of comments/links/parent through the production path.
- 822a AC2 → every NOT-SYNCED / "as expected" probe guard converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes (xfail) or removed.
- 822a AC3 → hermetic round-trip in CI; live variant opt-in/documented (matches SC4's "real Jira shapes asserted").
- 822a AC4 → applies the anti-change-detector posture (round-trip outcomes, not whole-blob equality) to its own tests, contributing to SC5.

All other parent criteria this child does not own are correctly delegated to named siblings (SC1/AC1/AC2 → 8937 + fe57 + 59ef; SC2/AC3 → 33d0; SC3/AC4 → 2359). No coverage finding is attributable to child 822a itself; its declared scope (round-trip + probe de-encoding; producer↔consumer schema explicitly OUT → story 8937) is consistent.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: SC4 + AC5 FULLY covered by this child; SC1/SC2/SC3/AC1/AC2/AC3/AC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below). No per-criterion gap attributable to 822a.

One ROSTER-LEVEL absence (not this child's fault; already raised on siblings 33d0 and 59ef — restated here for this pairing's record, and re-cross-checked against the whole roster INCLUDING this child):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail governs new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including 822a, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES the documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 822a AC4 "Assertions check round-trip outcomes, not whole-blob equality (anti-change-detector)" is self-scoped to this story's own tests; 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 8937 AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57 are likewise self-scoped; no sibling defines a documented guardrail that governs new/future tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone) or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

Repo corroboration:
- The probe 822a de-encodes exists verbatim: scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh L1542-1547 encodes the inbound-comment gap as expected — `fail_test "Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap" "unexpectedly synced inbound"` on the SYNCED branch and `pass_test "...inbound NOT implemented, as expected"` + `matrix_set "comments" "inbound" "update" "NOT-SYNCED"` (L1547) on the not-synced branch; L1914 documents it as "inbound NOT implemented — known gap, shown as NOT-SYNCED." A repo search for `issuelinks` under scripts/jira-pressure-test returned NO matches — corroborating 822a's premise that "no probe round-trips relationships at all."
- The bidirectional behavior 822a's hermetic round-trip asserts is ALREADY present in production code (the epic closes the TEST/PROBE-coverage seam, not re-fixes the bugs): inbound_differ._diff_comments_inbound reads fields["comment"]["comments"] (the singular-`comment`-key fix, L357-386); inbound link diff is wired (inbound_differ.py L712 `_diff_links_inbound`, with fetcher merging client.get_issuelinks_map into the snapshot at fetcher.py L450-451); and the production batch path already dispatches outbound link adds (batch_dispatch.py update_one L522-559, bug-3f04 fix). So 822a's comment/link/parent round-trip can be asserted against the current production path without an unmet sibling-delivered fix.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 822a's file_impact is scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh (de-encode guards) + tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_roundtrip.py (new). No sibling touches the probe script; the outbound-applier cluster (33d0/2359 → apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py) and the fixture/schema/contract files (fe57/8937/59ef) are disjoint from 822a's edits. No shared-file co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 822a assumes comments/links/parent round-trip through "the production path"; repo confirms inbound `comment`/`issuelinks` reads and outbound batch link dispatch already exist, so the assumption is consistent with fe57 (fixtures flow through fetch_snapshot unmassaged), 8937 (snapshot-entry schema), and 33d0 (production applier). No representation contradiction on the surfaces 822a exercises (the labels list-vs-count mismatch flagged on 2359/33d0 is outbound-telemetry-internal and untouched by 822a's round-trip).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 822a's only input dependency — story A's fixtures/fake — is correctly declared via depends_on fe57. The producer↔consumer schema/contract (8937) is explicitly scoped OUT and is not required to stand up a behavioral round-trip. The bug fixes its round-trip exercises are already in code, so 822a has no unmet "fix-must-land-first" edge to a sibling.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN (benign-reading filter). 33d0 AC2 (one OUTBOUND-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect = consolidation correctness) vs 822a AC1 (BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP) exercise the same production path from distinct angles — directionality + round-trip vs single-direction application — complementary, not duplicative. 822a also explicitly scopes the schema/contract OUT (→ 8937), so no overlap with the keystone.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 822a's edited files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster, so it is not entangled in that ordering hazard. Because the links/comments/issuelinks fixes its round-trip relies on already exist in production, no hard "33d0-before-822a" sequence is required for 822a's assertions to be meaningful; the only ordering edge (fixtures before the test that uses them) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 822a AC3 keeps the hermetic round-trip in CI and any live variant opt-in/scheduled/documented (consistent with parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests" and the epic's "live e2e is overkill / never flakes CI" posture); AC4 asserts round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references — BENIGN. The `Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap … NOT-SYNCED` guard 822a converts exists verbatim in the repo; no sibling deletes the probe scripts under scripts/jira-pressure-test, so its audit target is live. 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf applier does not strand anything 822a references (822a drives the production path, not the typed leaf).

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set and on this ticket's prior G3 audits — it is not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 822a): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent SC "Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a AC1 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip of comments/links/parent through the production path, no live Jira) + AC2 (every NOT-SYNCED/"as expected" probe guard converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes / removed) + AC3 (hermetic round-trip in CI; documented opt-in live variant for the real-Jira directions). FULLY.
- Parent AC "Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically through the production path" → 822a AC1. FULLY.
- Parent AC "Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" → 822a AC2 (audit scripts/jira-pressure-test/*.sh for every NOT-SYNCED / "as expected" guard; convert to xfail/fail-when-the-gap-closes or remove; none silently passes on a defect). FULLY.
- CONTRIBUTED: parent SC "new tests assert behavior/contract … zero change-detector tests added" via 822a AC4 (assertions check round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality); parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" via the in-CI hermetic round-trip (AC3) staying green.

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT item (producer↔consumer schema/contract → story B/8937) is consistent with the roster (and with 822a's declared depends_on fe57 for the fixtures/fake):
- AC SSOT snapshot-entry schema + AC fetcher-snapshot-validated-vs-schema + AC each-differ-validated-vs-schema + AC one round-trip contract test through the production path using real-Jira fixtures + SC producer/consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake shape fidelity).
- AC outbound collapsed to a single production path + AC retired outbound applier path deleted + SC outbound exercised through the production path, not a dead path → 33d0.
- AC outbound sub-op telemetry surfaced in the structured batch outcome + AC computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly (warn-first) + SC silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary → 2359.

ANTI-FP — the recurring "Parent AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) only PARTIALLY covered" finding from the earlier audits on THIS ticket is a FALSE POSITIVE and is WITHDRAWN. The actual parent acceptance criterion reads verbatim: "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)." It (a) explicitly NAMES its owner — story B = sibling 8937 — and (b) is FULLY covered there: 8937's description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests — a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it," and 8937 AC5 = "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests." Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts), this is FULLY covered, not partial. The earlier "AC6 PARTIALLY" finding was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text and predates/ignored 8937's explicit ownership; it is not re-filed. (The most recent G3 audits on siblings 2359/33d0/59ef already withdrew the same false positive.)

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY — parent SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" + parent AC "hermetic bidirectional round-trip" + parent AC "every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded" (all OWNED by THIS child); CONTRIBUTED — parent SC "zero change-detector" (822a AC4) + parent AC "suite green" (in-CI round-trip); COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — all schema/contract/round-trip (8937+fe57+59ef), applier-collapse/retired-path-deleted (33d0), telemetry/canary (2359), and the documented guardrail (8937 AC5, explicitly named owner); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent AC "all six closed; full reconciler suite green." UNCOVERED — none. No G3 coverage finding for this pairing.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The live e2e probe encoded the inbound-comment bug as EXPECTED behavior (`Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap … NOT-SYNCED`, and would have FAILED if comments synced), so the defect was undetectable; no probe round-trips relationships at all; and the probes are manual, not in CI. A probe that locks a bug in as "expected" is worse than no probe.

## What

- A **hermetic bidirectional round-trip assertion** — using story A's fixtures/fake through the production path — for comments, issue-links (both directions), and parent, runnable in CI without live Jira.
- **De-encode the "expected-bug" guards**: convert probe assertions that lock in a current gap (e.g. `Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap NOT-SYNCED`) into `xfail`/fail-when-the-gap-closes, so a fix is detected rather than rejected. Audit `scripts/jira-pressure-test/*.sh` for every `NOT-SYNCED`/"as expected" guard.
- A documented (optionally scheduled) live round-trip variant for the directions that truly need real Jira, kept off the default suite so it never flakes CI.

## Scope

- In: the hermetic round-trip test; the probe de-encoding audit; the live-variant doc.
- Out: the producer↔consumer schema/contract (story B).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A hermetic test asserts bidirectional round-trip of comments, links, and parent through the production path (no live Jira needed).
- [ ] Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as "expected/NOT-SYNCED" is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes (xfail) or removed; none silently passes on a defect.
- [ ] The hermetic round-trip runs in CI; any live variant is opt-in/scheduled and documented.
- [ ] Assertions check round-trip outcomes, not whole-blob equality (anti-change-detector).
